rag_rules: Fix unresolved property references by removing invalid AGREE clauses that reference AADL properties like HM_Errors or Partition_Slots.
Resolve type errors by ensuring AGREE expressions use valid types (int, real, bool) and visible identifiers.
Avoid placing implementation-level guarantees in component type annexes; move valid assigns to implementation scopes.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all identifiers are visible in the current scope and properly typed.
Use only declared constants, variables, or helper equations in AGREE expressions to prevent '<error>' type issues.
Place component type contracts in type scopes and implementation bindings in implementation scopes to avoid scoping errors.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks with proper `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, and `const` syntax.
Helper equations and constants must be declared before use and match expected types (int, real, bool).
Assignments in implementation scope must reference valid output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL property names like Stack_Size or HM_Errors directly in AGREE expressions; these are not automatically available as variables.
Do not place implementation-level guarantees in component type annexes; this causes scoping conflicts.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and their resolution in model scope
Component implementation bindings and subcomponent path references
ARINC653-specific property definitions and recovery actions
